items.summary:
Many of the rumors surrounding the next-generation Xbox are, at least seemingly, coming true. Microsoft and the newly appointed head of Xbox have already confirmed Project Helix is well into development and that it will indeed be able to play both Xbox and PC games. What that means exactly is still unclear, but we do have new details directly from Xbox on everything from a hint at the release window, the machine’s dual-functionality, and even some actual specs here today.

You’re looking at clearance pricing here as Acer has already rolled out newer models, including a 27-inch 240Hz version that is currently selling for $250. Today’s deal chops $120 off its $250 list price, landing at roughly a 48% discount. Not only is this the lowest price we can currently find for this model, but it’s also a solid price for a WQHD gaming display – it doesn’t get much less than this for a QHD monitor. This is a great option to consider if you’re putting together a budget-friendly battlestation, and you can learn more about it below.

items.title: Revive batteries, inflate tires, charge devices, and more with this GOOLOO A3 jump starter for $70
items.summary:
GOOLOO A3 Jump Starter with Air Compressor

Buy for $70 ($110 list price)

What we love

Not only are you gaining a handy means to jump start dead car batteries with the GOOLOO A3 model, which is particularly useful during extreme winter and summer weather, but you’re also getting a built-in air compressor here for your tires, too. It’s able to hit a max 150 PSI through its five different modes tailored for specific tire types (SUV, car, bike, ball, and DIY), and even comes with added functionality to act as a power bank, LED flashlight, and an S.O.S. emergency signal.
